Impact linked to private cloud incident.
Single platform outage affected hosted service.
Legacy estate now stable but still monitored.

supportOperations.commentary: January carry-over remained controlled despite elevated intake.
Volumes returned to expected seasonal levels.
One incident month but service metrics remained within tolerance.
Backlog reduced materially after workflow tidy-up.
Seasonal onboarding volumes increased but service stayed strong.
Best combined service and CSAT month in the period.

changeRelease.commentary: January change load elevated but broadly controlled.
One emergency change caused minor user disruption.
Release quality improved after CAB tightening.
No major user impact from change activity.
Strongest release month with controlled failure rate.

devDelivery.commentary: Backlog reduced but oldest item still distorting picture.
Backlog reduction slowed; oldest items still need governance focus.
New demand rose faster than throughput this month.
Backlog and age profile both improved.
Best delivery month in the period with fewer blocked items.

topRisks.riskIssue: Ageing laptop estate could still drive avoidable incidents if Q3 refresh slips
topRisks.currentControlMitigation: Prepare order and protect budget envelope
topRisks.commentary: Needs timely funding and supplier slot.

topRisks.riskIssue: Microsoft renewal remains a material H2 commercial event
topRisks.currentControlMitigation: Early market engagement and optimisation work
topRisks.commentary: Decision needed on commercial route and term.

topRisks.riskIssue: Historic development backlog may continue to distort confidence and stakeholder trust
topRisks.commentary: Requires business sponsorship and close criteria.

topRisks.riskIssue: Private cloud remains a residual resilience concern despite recent improvement
topRisks.currentControlMitigation: Monitor, harden and review rationalisation options

topRisks.riskIssue: Data ownership inconsistency could slow exec reporting maturity
topRisks.currentControlMitigation: Define named data owners by domain

topRisks.riskIssue: Patch compliance could regress if operational capacity is diverted into project work
topRisks.currentControlMitigation: Maintain monthly patch cadence and reporting

narrativeNotes.headline: Core service availability strongest in the half-year
narrativeNotes.narrative: June delivered excellent availability across network, cloud and critical platforms with no major incidents.

narrativeNotes.headline: Support performance improved while ticket CSAT remained high
narrativeNotes.narrative: Average resolution time reduced to 1.2 days and CSAT improved to 4.8/5.

narrativeNotes.headline: Critical vulnerability backlog reduced to zero
narrativeNotes.narrative: Patch compliance and remediation controls are now materially stronger than at the start of the year.

narrativeNotes.headline: Refresh progress still depends on protected Q3 funding
narrativeNotes.narrative: Lifecycle health has improved, but progress will stall without the next refresh wave.

narrativeNotes.headline: Licensing remains the main commercial pressure line
narrativeNotes.narrative: The position is improving, but optimisation work must continue before renewal.

narrativeNotes.headline: Approve Q3 device refresh spend envelope
narrativeNotes.narrative: Approving the refresh envelope now protects supplier lead times and supports further reduction in aged-estate incidents.

narrativeNotes.headline: Delivery confidence is improving across the active portfolio
narrativeNotes.narrative: Project CSAT and delivery confidence both improved after governance tightening.
